title: Phase 1 Ownership Compliance Audit — Full Agent Prompt Scan
scope: All agent prompts under monitor/prompts/ and monitor/prompts/reference/, cross-checked against build.js OWNERSHIP object and workspace-sync.md OWNED_BY_GIT array
methodology: Read every agent prompt file, extracted every file write operation (fs.writeFileSync, fs.appendFileSync, cp, echo >, overwrite instructions), then cross-checked each write against the OWNERSHIP table in build.js (lines 43-99) and the OWNED_BY_GIT array in workspace-sync.md (lines 56-75). Verified three-way consistency: CLAUDE.md ↔ build.js ↔ workspace-sync.md.

discrepancies_claude_md_vs_build_js.impact: build.js publish does NOT copy data/predictions.json git→workspace. Workspace FUSE copy can go stale. Agents reading from workspace (integrity, social) see stale predictions data.
discrepancies_claude_md_vs_build_js.recommended_fix: Add 'data/predictions.json': 'git' to build.js OWNERSHIP object (after 'data/uncounted-failures.json' line)

discrepancies_claude_md_vs_build_js.impact: build.js publish does NOT copy docs/llms.txt git→workspace. Social/integrity agents reading from workspace see stale version after decider commits an update.
Same as docs/llms.txt — no git→workspace sync on publish.

Analyst writes issue proposals to FUSE workspace. workspace-sync never copies them to git. Decider reads from workspace cwd (not clone), so the handoff works in practice. But proposals have no git persistence — if FUSE recycles before decider processes them, they're lost. Also no historical record.
discrepancies_claude_md_vs_build_js.recommended_fix: Add sync_glob monitor/analyst/issue-proposals '*.json' to workspace-sync.md (after line 158, the globe-fingerprints sync), and add 'monitor/analyst/issue-proposals/': 'append_only' to build.js OWNERSHIP

unclassified_files.recommendation: Document alongside tracker.json in CLAUDE.md's Unclassified section. Same protections apply: scheduling discipline, pre-push integrity gate, git merge-conflict detection. Already synced by workspace-sync (line 159).

unclassified_files.classification: Effectively git-owned (decider is sole writer from clone). workspace-sync copies FUSE→git (line 160) but this is a no-op when decider's git version is newer.
unclassified_files.recommendation: Consider classifying as git-owned in build.js since decider is the sole writer. OR document as multi-writer if analyst also writes (not observed in current prompts).

unclassified_files.classification: workspace-only (not synced to git, not consumed from git)
unclassified_files.recommendation: Low priority. Social reports are only read by tinker from FUSE. Could add sync_glob for persistence but not functionally required.

unclassified_files.classification: workspace-only convenience file. Read by decider-intake.md (line 9).
unclassified_files.recommendation: Should be synced to git so decider can read from clone. Currently decider reads from workspace cwd, which works. Low priority.

unclassified_files.classification: Build artifact. Already synced by workspace-sync (line 167).
unclassified_files.recommendation: No action needed — already synced correctly.

unclassified_files.classification: workspace-only convenience file in an append_only directory. Overwrite semantics conflict with append_only classification.
unclassified_files.recommendation: Low priority. The overwrite doesn't break anything because append_only only governs the sync direction, not the filesystem. The file is a convenience summary consumed from FUSE by decider.

unclassified_files.classification: Workspace-only convenience file in an append_only directory. Overwrite semantics. Already synced by workspace-sync (sync_glob line 171 handles *.txt).
workspace-only convenience file in append_only_glob dir.

written_by: human operator (dual-write to FUSE + git clone)
unclassified_files.classification: Dual-write per CLAUDE.md rule. Not in OWNERSHIP table.
unclassified_files.recommendation: Deliberate dual-write — documenting in CLAUDE.md is sufficient. Adding to OWNERSHIP would break the dual-write pattern.

writes: monitor/changes/latest-poll-summary.txt (overwrite in append_only dir — functional, synced)
monitor/analyst/issue-proposals/proposal-*.json (unclassified but correct direction — FUSE write for FUSE consumer)
clean_agents.violations: none — prior open-issues.json violation CONFIRMED FIXED (no grep hits for open-issues in analyst prompts)
none — prior priority-queue.json violation CONFIRMED FIXED (line 160: 'Do NOT modify priority-queue.json')

writes: docs/ files via cp from social drafts (git ✓)

clean_agents.violations: none — prior docs/ direct-write violation CONFIRMED FIXED (social.md line 234: explicit Phase 1 ownership warning)

writes: monitor/integrity/workspace-sync-skips.jsonl (git ✓, via fs.appendFileSync bypassing smart_copy direction guard)
Various cp operations governed by smart_copy direction guard

false_alarms.concern: workspace-sync writes to a git-owned file — looks like a violation
false_alarms.resolution: By design. workspace-sync is the authoritative writer via fs.appendFileSync, bypassing its own smart_copy direction guard. The 'git' classification in build.js ensures publish copies the file to FUSE workspace for integrity to read. Documented in build.js comments (lines 57-73).

false_alarms.concern: Multi-writer (curmudgeon from FUSE + decider from git clone)
false_alarms.resolution: Deliberately unclassified per build.js comment (lines 79-85) and CLAUDE.md line 69. Protected by scheduling discipline, pre-push integrity gate, and git merge-conflict detection. Deferred to Phase 2 shard split.

false_alarms.concern: Multi-writer (analyst from FUSE + decider from git clone)
false_alarms.resolution: Same class as tracker.json. Both writers are protected by non-concurrent scheduling. workspace-sync smart_copy has mtime-vs-git-time guard. Should be documented as multi-writer in CLAUDE.md.

summary.highest_priority_fix: Add data/predictions.json, docs/llms.txt, docs/sitemap.xml, docs/robots.txt to build.js OWNERSHIP as 'git'. These are in CLAUDE.md and workspace-sync OWNED_BY_GIT but missing from build.js, meaning publish doesn't sync them git→workspace.
summary.second_priority_fix: Add monitor/analyst/issue-proposals/ to both build.js OWNERSHIP (append_only) and workspace-sync sync_glob for persistence.
summary.third_priority: Document expansion-tracker.json as multi-writer alongside tracker.json in CLAUDE.md.

verification_pattern: grep -q "'data/predictions.json'" build.js && grep -q "'docs/llms.txt'" build.js && grep -q "issue-proposals" monitor/prompts/workspace-sync.md && echo FIXED || echo STILL_BROKEN
